# jschon

{{ python }} {{ pypi }} {{ docs }} {{ tests }} {{ codecov }} {{ license }} {{ downloads }}

A JSON toolkit for Python developers.

## Features

- JSON Schema validator implementation
  ([drafts 2019-09, 2020-12](https://json-schema.org/))

  - Schema compilation and indexing
  - \$ref loading from local and remote sources
  - Support for custom keywords, vocabularies and meta-schemas
  - Support for format validation

- JSON class implementing the JSON data model

- JSON Pointer ([RFC 6901](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6901.html))

- JSON Patch ([RFC 6902](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6902.html))

- Relative JSON Pointer ([draft](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-bhutton-relative-json-pointer-00))

## Installation

```
pip install jschon
```

For remote \$ref support, the requests library is required. It may be installed with:

```
pip install jschon[requests]
```

## Basic usage

Create a JSON schema:

```python
from jschon import create_catalog, JSON, JSONSchema

create_catalog('2020-12')

demo_schema = JSONSchema({
    "$id": "https://example.com/demo",
    "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
    "type": "array",
    "items": {
        "anyOf": [
            {
                "type": "string",
                "description": "Cool! We got a string here!"
            },
            {
                "type": "integer",
                "description": "Hey! We got an integer here!"
            }
        ]
    }
})
```

Validate JSON data:

```python
result = demo_schema.evaluate(
    JSON([12, "Monkeys"])
)
```

Generate JSON Schema-conformant output:

```pycon
>>> result.output('basic')
{
    "valid": True,
    "annotations": [
        {
            "instanceLocation": "",
            "keywordLocation": "/items",
            "absoluteKeywordLocation": "https://example.com/demo#/items",
            "annotation": True
        },
        {
            "instanceLocation": "/0",
            "keywordLocation": "/items/anyOf/1/description",
            "absoluteKeywordLocation": "https://example.com/demo#/items/anyOf/1/description",
            "annotation": "Hey! We got an integer here!"
        },
        {
            "instanceLocation": "/1",
            "keywordLocation": "/items/anyOf/0/description",
            "absoluteKeywordLocation": "https://example.com/demo#/items/anyOf/0/description",
            "annotation": "Cool! We got a string here!"
        }
    ]
}
```

## Links

- [Documentation](https://jschon.readthedocs.io)
- [Package info](https://pypi.org/project/jschon)
- [Source code](https://github.com/marksparkza/jschon)

functions.name: jschon.utils.json_dumpf
documentation: Serialize a JSON-compatible Python object to a JSON file.

functions.name: jschon.utils.json_dumps
documentation: Serialize a JSON-compatible Python object to a JSON string.

functions.name: jschon.utils.json_loadf
documentation: Deserialize a JSON file, returning a JSON-compatible Python object.

functions.name: jschon.utils.json_loadr
documentation: Fetch and deserialize a remote JSON resource, returning a JSON-compatible Python object.

functions.name: jschon.utils.json_loads
documentation: Deserialize a JSON string, returning a JSON-compatible Python object.

functions.name: jschon.utils._parse_invalid_const
documentation:
Called when '-Infinity', 'Infinity' or 'NaN' is encountered in the
JSON-encoded input. These JavaScript constants are not strictly allowed
by the JSON data model.

functions.name: jschon.vocabulary.format.format_validator
documentation:
A decorator for a format validation function.

The decorator only registers a function as a format validator.
Assertion behaviour must be enabled in a catalog using
{meth}`~jschon.catalog.Catalog.enable_formats`.

```
param format_attr

The format attribute that the decorated function validates.

param instance_types

The set of instance types validated by this format.
```

variables.name: jschon.vocabulary.format.FormatValidator
documentation:
Call signature for a function decorated with {func}`format_validator`.

The function validates a JSON-compatible Python object (typically, a string)
and raises a {exc}`ValueError` if the object is invalid per the applicable
format specification.

classes.name: jschon.vocabulary.Metaschema

methods.name: jschon.vocabulary.Metaschema.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a {class}`Metaschema` instance from the given
schema-compatible `value`.

```
param catalog

catalog instance

param value

a schema-compatible Python object

param default_core_vocabulary

the metaschema's
core , used in the absence
of a "$vocabulary" keyword in the metaschema JSON file, or
if a known core vocabulary is not present under "$vocabulary"

param default_vocabulary

default 
instances, used in the absence of a "$vocabulary" keyword in the
metaschema JSON file

param kwargs

additional keyword arguments to pass through to the
 constructor

raise JSONSchemaError

if no core vocabulary can be determined

raise CatalogError

if the created metaschema is not valid
```

methods.name: jschon.vocabulary.Metaschema.get_kwclass
documentation:
Return the {class}`Keyword` class this metaschema uses for the given key.
If the key is not recognized, a subclass of an internal {class}`Keyword`
subclass that treats the keyword as a simple annotation is automatically
created, associated with the key, and returned.

A metaschema declares the set of vocabularies that are available
to any schema which references it, and provides any such schema with
its {class}`Keyword` classes.

{class}`Metaschema` is itself a subclass of {class}`~jschon.jsonschema.JSONSchema`,
and may be used to validate any referencing schema.

classes.name: jschon.vocabulary.Vocabulary

methods.name: jschon.vocabulary.Vocabulary.__repr__
documentation: Return `repr(self)`.

A vocabulary declares a set of keywords that may be used in the
evaluation of JSON documents, and provides a runtime implementation
(in the form of a {class}`Keyword` class) for each of those keywords.

class_variables.name: jschon.vocabulary.Keyword.key
documentation: The keyword name as it appears in a schema object.

class_variables.name: jschon.vocabulary.Keyword.instance_types
documentation: The types of instance that the keyword can evaluate.

class_variables.name: jschon.vocabulary.Keyword.depends_on
documentation: Keywords that must be evaluated before this keyword can be evaluated.

class_variables.name: jschon.vocabulary.Keyword.static
documentation:
`static = True` (equivalent to `instance_types = ()`) indicates that the keyword
does not ever evaluate any instance.

classes.name: jschon.vocabulary.Subschema
documentation:
A {class}`~Keyword` class mixin that sets up a subschema for
a keyword.

classes.name: jschon.vocabulary.ArrayOfSubschemas
documentation:
A {class}`~Keyword` class mixin that sets up an array of subschemas
for a keyword.

classes.name: jschon.vocabulary.ObjectOfSubschemas
documentation:
A {class}`~Keyword` class mixin that sets up property-based subschemas
for a keyword.

functions.name: jschon.output.output_formatter
documentation:
A decorator for a JSON Schema output formatting function.

```
param format

A string identifying the output format.
```

variables.name: jschon.output.OutputFormatter
documentation:
Call signature for a function decorated with {func}`output_formatter`.

The function must take a {class}`~jschon.jsonschema.Result` object
as its first argument.

modules.name: jschon.exceptions
documentation: noinspection PyUnresolvedReferences

functions.name: jschon.create_catalog
documentation:
Create and return a {class}`~jschon.catalog.Catalog` instance,
initialized with a meta-schema and keyword support for each of the
specified JSON Schema `versions`.

```
param versions

Any of 2019-09, 2020-12, next.

param name

A unique name for the  instance.

raise ValueError

If any of  is unrecognized.
```

classes.name: jschon.exc.JschonError
documentation: Generic error class.

classes.name: jschon.exc.JSONError
documentation: An error originating in the {class}`~jschon.json` module.

classes.name: jschon.exc.JSONPointerError
documentation: An error originating in the {mod}`~jschon.jsonpointer` module.

classes.name: jschon.exc.JSONPointerMalformedError
documentation:
Raised for an invalid {class}`~jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer`
constructor argument.

classes.name: jschon.exc.JSONPointerReferenceError
documentation:
Raised when a {class}`~jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer`
evaluates a non-existent location in a document.

classes.name: jschon.exc.RelativeJSONPointerMalformedError
documentation:
Raised for an invalid {class}`~jschon.jsonpointer.RelativeJSONPointer`

classes.name: jschon.exc.RelativeJSONPointerReferenceError
documentation:
Raised when a {class}`~jschon.jsonpointer.RelativeJSONPointer`
evaluates a non-existent location in a document.

classes.name: jschon.exc.JSONSchemaError
documentation:
Raised when an error occurs during construction of a
{class}`~jschon.jsonschema.JSONSchema` object. May be raised by
{class}`~jschon.vocabulary.Keyword` initializers and reference
resolution methods.

classes.name: jschon.exc.URIError
documentation: An error originating in the {mod}`~jschon.uri` module.

methods.name: jschon.uri.URI.resolve
documentation: Produce a new URI by resolving self against the given base URI.

methods.name: jschon.uri.URI.copy
documentation:
Produce a new URI composed of the specified components of self.

- True => use existing
- False/None => remove
- Otherwise => replace

methods.name: jschon.uri.URI.validate
documentation:
Validate self.

:raise URIError: if self fails validation

variables.name: jschon.json.JSONCompatible
documentation: Type hint for a JSON-compatible Python object.

documentation: Use to represent the JSON `null` value literally in Python code.

variables.name: jschon.json.true
documentation: Use to represent the JSON `true` value literally in Python code.

variables.name: jschon.json.false
documentation: Use to represent the JSON `false` value literally in Python code.

```
the path to the file

keyword arguments to pass to the  (subclass) constructor
```

```
the JSON string

keyword arguments to pass to the  (subclass) constructor
```

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a {class}`JSON` instance from the given JSON-compatible
`value`.

The `parent`, `key`, `itemclass` and `itemkwargs` parameters should
typically only be used in the construction of compound {class}`JSON`
documents by {class}`JSON` subclasses.

```
param value

a JSON-compatible Python object

param parent

the parent node of the instance

param key

the index of the instance within its parent

param itemclass

the  subclass used to instantiate
child nodes of arrays and objects (default: )

param itemkwargs

keyword arguments to pass to the 
constructor
```

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.path
documentation: Return the path to the instance from the document root.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.value
documentation: Return the instance data as a JSON-compatible Python object.

documentation:
Serialize the instance data to a JSON file.

```
param path

the path to the file
```

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.dumps
documentation: Serialize the instance data to a JSON string.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.__repr__
documentation: Return `repr(self)`.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.__str__
documentation: Return `str(self)`.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.__bool__
documentation: Return `bool(self)`.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.__len__
documentation:
Return `len(self)`.

Supported for JSON types `string`, `array` and `object`.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.__iter__
documentation:
Return `iter(self)`.

Supported for JSON types `array` and `object`.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.__getitem__
documentation:
Return `self[index]`.

Supported for JSON types `array` and `object`.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.__setitem__
documentation:
Set `self[index]` to `obj`.

Supported for JSON types `array` and `object`.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.__delitem__
documentation:
Delete `self[index]`.

Supported for JSON types `array` and `object`.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.insert
documentation:
Insert `obj` before `index`.

Supported for JSON type `array`.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.__eq__
documentation: Return `self == other`.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.__ge__
documentation:
Return `self >= other`.

Supported for JSON types `number` and `string`.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.__gt__
documentation:
Return `self > other`.

Supported for JSON types `number` and `string`.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.__le__
documentation:
Return `self <= other`.

Supported for JSON types `number` and `string`.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.__lt__
documentation:
Return `self < other`.

Supported for JSON types `number` and `string`.

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.add
documentation:
Add `obj` at `path` relative to `self`.

The {class}`JSON` equivalent to {func}`~jschon.jsonpatch.add`,
this method performs an in-place JSON Patch `add` operation on `self`.

If `path` is empty, the value of `self` is replaced by `obj`.

*Experimental.*

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.remove
documentation:
Remove the instance at `path` relative to `self`.

The {class}`JSON` equivalent to {func}`~jschon.jsonpatch.remove`,
this method performs an in-place JSON Patch `remove` operation on `self`.

If `path` is empty, the value of `self` is set to `None`.

*Experimental.*

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.replace
documentation:
Set `obj` at `path` relative to `self`.

The {class}`JSON` equivalent to {func}`~jschon.jsonpatch.replace`,
this method performs an in-place JSON Patch `replace` operation on `self`.

If `path` is empty, the value of `self` is replaced by `obj`.

*Experimental.*

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.move
documentation: *Not yet implemented; experimental.*

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.copy
documentation: *Not yet implemented; experimental.*

methods.name: jschon.json.JSON.test
documentation: *Not yet implemented; experimental.*
An implementation of the JSON data model.

functions.name: jschon.jsonpatch.add
documentation: Add `value` to `document` at `path`.

functions.name: jschon.jsonpatch.remove
documentation: Remove the value at `path` in `document`.

functions.name: jschon.jsonpatch.replace
documentation: Replace the value at `path` in `document` with `value`.

functions.name: jschon.jsonpatch.move
documentation: Move the value at `from_` in `document` to `path`.

functions.name: jschon.jsonpatch.copy
documentation: Copy the value at `from_` in `document` to `path`.

functions.name: jschon.jsonpatch.test
documentation: Test whether the value at `path` in `document` is equal to `value`.

classes.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatchOperation

methods.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatchOperation.__new__
documentation:
Create and return a new {class}`JSONPatchOperation` instance.

```
param op

The operation to perform. One of add, remove,
replace, move, copy, test.

param path

A JSON pointer to the target location.

param value

For add and replace operations, the value
to set at the target location. For test, the value to
compare with the target.

param from

The location from which to move or copy.
An alias for , which may be passed via .
```

methods.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatchOperation.apply
documentation:
Apply the patch operation to `document` and return the
resultant document.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatchOperation.asdict
documentation: Return `self` as a dict.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatchOperation.__eq__
documentation: Return `self == other`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatchOperation.__repr__
documentation: Return `repr(self)`.
{rfc}`6902`-conformant JSON patch operation object.

classes.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatch

methods.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatch.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a {class}`JSONPatch` instance from the given `operations`,
each of which may be a {class}`JSONPatchOperation` or a JSON patch
operation-conformant dictionary.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatch.evaluate
documentation:
Return the result of sequentially applying all patch operations
to `document`, as a new document. `document` itself is not modified.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatch.aslist
documentation: Return `self` as a list of operation dicts.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatch.__getitem__
documentation: Return `self[index]`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatch.__setitem__
documentation: Set `self[index]` to `operation`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatch.__delitem__
documentation: Delete `self[index]`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatch.__len__
documentation: Return `len(self)`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatch.insert
documentation: Insert `operation` before `index`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatch.__eq__
documentation: Return `self == other`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpatch.JSONPatch.__repr__
documentation: Return `repr(self)`.
{rfc}`6902`-conformant JSON Patch implementation.

classes.name: jschon.jsonschema.JSONSchema

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.JSONSchema.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a {class}`JSONSchema` instance from the given
schema-compatible `value`.

```
param value

a schema-compatible Python object

param catalog

catalog instance or catalog name

param cacheid

schema cache identifier

param uri

the URI identifying the schema; an "$id" keyword
appearing in  will override this

param metaschema_uri

the URI identifying the schema's metaschema;
a "$schema" keyword appearing in  will override this

param parent

the parent node of the schema; used internally when
creating a subschema

param key

the index of the schema within its parent; used internally
when creating a subschema
```

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.JSONSchema.validate
documentation: Validate the schema against its metaschema.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.JSONSchema.evaluate
documentation:
Evaluate a JSON document and return the evaluation result.

```
param instance

the JSON document to evaluate

param result

the current result node; given by keywords
when invoking this method recursively
```

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.JSONSchema.parentschema
documentation:
The containing {class}`JSONSchema` instance.

Note that this is not necessarily the same as `self.parent`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.JSONSchema.resource_rootschema
documentation:
The {class}`JSONSchema` at the root of the containing resource.

This is the nearest ancestor (including `self`) containing `"$id"`,
or if none exist, it is the same as `self.document_rootschema`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.JSONSchema.document_rootschema
documentation:
The {class}`JSONSchema` at the root of the entire document.

If no ancestor schemas contain `"$id"`, this is the same as
`self.resource_rootschema`.  If this schema has no `self.parentschema`,
this method returns `self`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.JSONSchema.metaschema
documentation: The schema's {class}`~jschon.vocabulary.Metaschema`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.JSONSchema.metaschema_uri
documentation:
The {class}`~jschon.uri.URI` identifying the schema's metaschema.

If not defined on this (sub)schema, the metaschema URI
is determined by the parent schema.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.JSONSchema.base_uri
documentation:
The schema's base {class}`~jschon.uri.URI`.

The base URI is obtained by searching up the schema tree
for a schema URI, and removing any fragment.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.JSONSchema.uri
documentation:
The {class}`~jschon.uri.URI` identifying the schema.

Used as the key for caching the schema in the catalog.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.JSONSchema.canonical_uri
documentation:
The absolute location of the (sub)schema.

This is not necessarily an 'absolute URI', as it may contain
a fragment.
JSON schema document model.

classes.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result.__call__
documentation:
Yield a subresult for the evaluation of `instance`.
Descend down the evaluation path by `key`, into `schema` if given, or
within `self.schema` otherwise.

Extension keywords may provide a custom {class}`Result` class via `cls`,
which is applied to all nodes within the yielded subtree.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result.schema_node
documentation: Return the current schema node.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result.sibling
documentation: Return a sibling schema node's evaluation result for `instance`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result.annotate
documentation: Annotate the result.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result.fail
documentation: Mark the result as invalid, optionally with an error.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result.pass_
documentation:
Mark the result as valid.

A result is initially valid, so this should only need
to be called by a keyword when it must reverse a failure.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result.noassert
documentation: Indicate that evaluation passes regardless of validity.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result.discard
documentation: Indicate that the result should be ignored and discarded.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result.refschema
documentation:
Set the referenced schema for a by-reference keyword.

This ensures that {attr}`absolute_uri` returns the URI of the
referenced schema rather than the referencing keyword.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result.valid
documentation: Return the validity of the instance against the schema.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result.passed
documentation:
Return the assertion result for the schema node.

In the standard JSON Schema vocabulary, this can only differ
from {attr}`valid` for the `if` keyword: validity may be false
(triggering `else`) while its assertion result is always true.

For the root result node, {attr}`passed` will always equal {attr}`valid`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result.absolute_uri
documentation: Return the absolute URI of the current schema node.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result.collect_annotations
documentation:
Return an iterator over annotations produced in this subtree,
optionally filtered by instance and/or keyword.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result.collect_errors
documentation:
Return an iterator over errors produced in this subtree,
optionally filtered by instance and/or keyword.

methods.name: jschon.jsonschema.Result.output
documentation:
Return the evaluation result in the specified `format`.

```
param format

One of the standard JSON Schema output formats --
flag, basic, detailed or verbose -- or any
format registered with the 
decorator.

param kwargs

Keyword arguments to pass to the output formatter.
```

The result of evaluating a JSON document
node against a JSON schema node.

The root of a {class}`Result` tree represents
a complete document evaluation result.

classes.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer

methods.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.__new__
documentation:
Create and return a new {class}`JSONPointer` instance, constructed by
the concatenation of the given `values`.

```
param values

each value may either be an RFC 6901 string, or an iterable
of unescaped keys

raise JSONPointerMalformedError

if a string argument does not conform to
the RFC 6901 syntax
```

methods.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.__getitem__
documentation: Return `self[index]`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.__len__
documentation: Return `len(self)`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.__truediv__
documentation: Return `self / suffix`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.__eq__
documentation: Return `self == other`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.__le__
documentation:
Return `self <= other`.

Test whether self is a prefix of other, that is,
`self == other[:len(self)]`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.__lt__
documentation:
Return `self < other`.

Test whether self is a proper prefix of other, that is,
`self <= other and self != other`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.__hash__
documentation: Return `hash(self)`.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.__str__

methods.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.evaluate
documentation:
Return the value within `document` at the location referenced by `self`.

`document` may be of any type, though if neither a {class}`Mapping` nor
a {class}`Sequence`, evaluation by any non-empty {class}`JSONPointer`
will always fail.

```
param document

any Python object

raise JSONPointerReferenceError

if  references a non-existent
location in 
```

methods.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.parse_uri_fragment
documentation:
Return a new {class}`JSONPointer` constructed from the {rfc}`6901`
string obtained by decoding `value`.

`value` must exclude the initial `'#'` of the fragment; this allows
for sensible interoperation with {class}`~jschon.uri.URI` objects.

```
param value

a percent-encoded URI fragment
```

methods.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.uri_fragment
documentation:
Return a percent-encoded URI fragment representation of `self`.

The returned string excludes the initial `'#'` of the fragment; this
allows for sensible interoperation with {class}`~jschon.uri.URI` objects.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.escape
documentation:
Return the escaped form of a JSON object key / array index,
suitable for use in an {rfc}`6901` JSON pointer string.

```
param key

an unescaped key
```

methods.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.unescape
documentation:
Return the unescaped form of a reference token appearing in an
{rfc}`6901` JSON pointer string

```
param token

an RFC 6901 reference token
```

class_variables.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.malformed_exc
documentation: Exception raised when the input is not a valid JSON Pointer.

class_variables.name: jschon.jsonpointer.JSONPointer.reference_exc
documentation: Exception raised when the JSON Pointer cannot be resolved against a document.

{rfc}`6901`-conformant JSON Pointer implementation.

A JSON pointer is a string representing a reference to some value within a
JSON document. It consists of a series of *reference tokens* each prefixed by
`"/"`, each token in turn being the (escaped) JSON object key or the JSON
array index at the next node down the path to the referenced value. The empty
string `""` represents a reference to an entire JSON document.

A {class}`JSONPointer` instance is an immutable sequence of the unescaped
JSON object keys [^footnote-1] and/or array indices [^footnote-2] that comprise the path to a
referenced value within a JSON document.

A {class}`JSONPointer` instance is constructed by the concatenation of any
number of arguments, each of which can be one of:

- a string conforming to the {rfc}`6901` syntax
- an iterable of unescaped keys (which may itself be a {class}`JSONPointer`
  instance)

Two {class}`JSONPointer` instances compare equal if their key sequences are
identical.

The `/` operator provides a convenient syntax for extending a JSON pointer.
It produces a new {class}`JSONPointer` instance by copying the left-hand
argument (a {class}`JSONPointer` instance) and appending the right-hand
argument (an unescaped key, or an iterable of unescaped keys).

Taking an index into a {class}`JSONPointer` returns the unescaped key at
that position. Taking a slice into a {class}`JSONPointer` returns a new
{class}`JSONPointer` composed of the specified slice of the original's keys.

[^footnote-1]: An *unescaped* object key is the unmodified form of the key within its
    mapping. Keys appearing in an RFC 6901 JSON pointer string, on the other
    hand, are required by the syntax to have reserved characters *escaped*.

[^footnote-2]: Object keys and array indices are uniformly represented as strings
    and referred to as *keys* in the JSONPointer class.

methods.name: jschon.jsonpointer.RelativeJSONPointer.__new__
documentation:
Create and return a new {class}`RelativeJSONPointer` instance.

```
param value

a relative JSON pointer-conformant string; if  is
given, keyword args are ignored

param up

the number of levels up from the current referenced JSON node
from which to evaluate 

param over

the integer value used to adjust the array index after
applying , which is only valid if that location is an array item;
a value of 0, which is not allowed by the grammar, is treated as if
there is no adjustment.

param ref

a  instance, or the literal '#'

raise RelativeJSONPointerMalformedError

for any invalid arguments
```

Return the value within `document` at the location referenced by `self`.

`document` must be an instance of {class}`JSON`, as evaluation relies

```

location in
```

```
a unique name for this  instance
```

methods.name: jschon.catalog.Catalog.add_uri_source
documentation:
Register a source for loading URI-identified JSON resources.

A base URI of `None` registers a default source that handles any
URI that does not match any registered base URI string.

```
param base_uri

a normalized, absolute URI - including scheme, without
a fragment, and ending with '/' or None to match complete URIs

param source

a  object

raise CatalogError

if  is invalid
```

methods.name: jschon.catalog.Catalog.load_json
documentation:
Load a JSON-compatible object from the source for `uri`.

If there are multiple candidate base URIs for `uri`, the most specific
match (i.e. the longest one) is selected.

```
param uri

a normalized, absolute URI - including scheme, without
a fragment

raise CatalogError

if  is invalid, a source is not available
for , or if a loading error occurs
```

documentation:

may be used by a {class}`~jschon.vocabulary.Metaschema` to provide

```

the URI identifying the vocabulary

param kwclasses

the  classes
constituting the vocabulary

returns

the newly created  instance
```

methods.name: jschon.catalog.Catalog.get_vocabulary
documentation:

```
param uri

the URI identifying the vocabulary

raise CatalogError
```

```
param uri

the URI identifying the metaschema

param default_core_vocabulary_uri

the URI identifying the metaschema's
core , used in the absence
of a "$vocabulary" keyword in the metaschema JSON file, or
if a known core vocabulary is not present under "$vocabulary"

param default_vocabulary_uris

default 
URIs, used in the absence of a "$vocabulary" keyword in the
metaschema JSON file

param kwargs

additional keyword arguments to pass through to the
 constructor

returns

raise CatalogError

if the metaschema is not valid
```

methods.name: jschon.catalog.Catalog.get_metaschema
documentation:
Get a metaschema identified by `uri` from the `'__meta__'` cache, or
load it from configured sources if not already cached.

Note that metaschemas that do not declare a known core vocabulary
in `"$vocabulary"` must first be created using {meth}`create_metaschema`.

```
param uri

the URI identifying the metaschema

raise CatalogError

if the object referenced by  is not
a , or if it is not valid

raise JSONSchemaError

if the metaschema is loaded from sources
but no known core vocabulary is present in "$vocabulary"
```

Enable validation of the specified format attributes.

These may include formats defined in {mod}`jschon.formats`
and elsewhere.

```
raise CatalogError

if a schema cannot be found for , or if the
object referenced by  is not a
```

methods.name: jschon.catalog.Catalog.cache
documentation:
Context manager for a schema cache.

Example usage:

```
with catalog.cache() as cacheid:
    schema = JSONSchema(..., cacheid=cacheid)
```

The cache and its contents are popped from the catalog
upon exiting the `with` block.

The {class}`Catalog` acts as a schema cache, enabling schemas and
subschemas to be indexed, re-used, and cross-referenced by URI.
